records.name: CCSDS Mission Operations Message Abstraction Layer (MAL)
records.summary: The CCSDS Mission Operations framework separates mission-operation services from implementation technology through a layered architecture: Mission Operations services, Common Services, Message Abstraction Layer, and transport bindings. MAL defines technology-neutral interaction patterns and permits protocol adapters so telemetry, control, event, archive, timeline, and bulk-data services can be integrated without hard-coding applications to a single middleware stack.
useful_for: mission control and station interface normalization
future integration with CCSDS-compliant mission systems
separation of operational services from transport protocols
records.confidence_level: High for architecture and terminology because CCSDS is the primary standards body; implementation-level compatibility remains system-specific.
pending_confirmation_fields: exact CCSDS MAL version supported by each operator
records.next_action: Add MAL as a separate interface family beside CCSDS SLE. Model service definitions, bindings, adapters and conformance as separate linked entities rather than one free-text interface field.

records.name: SCOS-2000 telemetry and SLE integration pattern
records.summary: ESA's SCOS-2000 ground-system model receives CCSDS transfer frames from ground stations through Space Link Extension interfaces, demultiplexes them into telemetry packets, performs parameter monitoring and limit checking, validates telecommands, and archives packets and parameters. This provides a practical reference architecture connecting station-delivered frames to mission-control telemetry processing and operational archives.
records.confidence_level: High for the general ESA ground-system processing pattern; medium for direct reuse because licensing, release, configuration and operator implementation details vary.
pending_confirmation_fields: licensing terms for the intended deployment jurisdiction
records.next_action: Use this pattern to split GS LinkOps AI delivery into RF reception, transfer-frame handover, packet extraction, telemetry validation, archive and customer delivery stages. Do not treat raw RF reception and mission-ready telemetry as the same service product.
